args.plan:
**What I found (OMO + OpenCode plugin API)**
- OMO implements a per-turn, UI-invisible reminder by mutating the last user message in `experimental.chat.messages.transform`:
  - `.loom/workspaces/repos/omo/src/hooks/phase-reminder/index.ts`
  - Wired in `.loom/workspaces/repos/omo/src/index.ts` as:
    - `experimental.chat.messages.transform` → prepends a reminder block to the user’s text part
  - Key property: it runs “right before sending to API”, so the reminder doesn’t clutter the UI, but is always in the model’s immediate attention.
- OMO also appends a visible nudge after specific tools via `tool.execute.after`:
  - `.loom/workspaces/repos/omo/src/hooks/post-read-nudge/index.ts`
  - It detects `Read/read` and appends a reminder string to `output.output`.
- OpenCode’s plugin events list (docs): `tool.execute.before/after`, `tui.prompt.append`, `command.executed`, `session.*`, etc. It also supports `experimental.session.compacting` to inject compaction context.

**Where Loom already is (relevant existing integration)**
- Loom already ships an OpenCode plugin template as part of compound scaffolding:
  - `src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts`
  - It already:
    - logs `tool.execute.before/after` + selected session events → `.opencode/memory/observations.jsonl`
    - triggers background autolearn on `session.idle`
    - injects compaction context via `experimental.session.compacting`
- Loom memory already supports command scopes (`scope kind: command`) and uses substring match for `command:` scopes:
  - `src/agent_loom/memory/constants.py` / `src/agent_loom/memory/scopes.py`
  - CLI supports `recall --command` as shorthand for `--scope command:...`, but `add/edit` currently require `--scope command:...`.

**Goal-aligned approach**
We’ll tackle this in two coordinated layers:
1) OpenCode-side “nudge rails” (per-turn + post-tool) to improve instruction-followi... (truncated, len=7003)
args.summary: Study OMO shows OpenCode supports per-turn invisible prompt injection via `experimental.chat.messages.transform` plus tool hooks (`tool.execute.before/after`) that can append nudges after specific tools (OMO uses Read). Loom already ships a Compound OpenCode plugin template that logs tool calls/events and runs autolearn; we can extend that template to inject a small Loom ticket+memory protocol reminder into every user turn and add targeted post-tool nudges that encourage scoped `loom memory` captures (especially `command:`) and ticket hygiene. In parallel, we can improve Loom’s memory CLI ergonomics by adding `--command` shorthand to `loom memory add/edit` (mirroring `recall --command`) so these nudges are frictionless and consistent.

 def add(
     *,
     vault: Optional[str] = None,
     title: Optional[str] = None,
     visibility: str = "shared",
     status: str = "active",
     tag: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
     alias: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
     scope: Optional[Sequence[str]] = None,
+    command: Optional[str] = None,
     allow_missing_scopes: bool = False,
     body: Optional[str] = None,
     interactive: bool = False,
     note_id: Optional[str] = None,
     folder: str = "",
 ) -> AddResult:
@@
-    scope_items = parse_multi_csvish(list(scope) if scope else None)
+    scope_items = parse_multi_csvish(list(scope) if scope else None)
+    if command is not None:
+        cmd = str(command).strip()
+        if not cmd:
+            raise MemoryError(
+                "--command cannot be empty",
+                code="ARG",
+                exit_code=2,
+                hint="Provide a non-empty command signature.",
+                suggestions=[
+                    'loom memory add --title "pytest failure" --command "uv run pytest" --body "..."',
+                ],
+            )
+        scope_items.append(f"command:{cmd}")
     scopes = [parse_scope_string(s, repo_root=repo_root) for s in scope_items]
+    if scopes:
+        # Dedup by JSON signature for stable frontmatter.
+        sigs = {json.dumps(s, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False): s for s in scopes}
+        scopes = list(sigs.values())
     validate_file_scopes_exist(
         scopes,
         repo_root=repo_root,
         cwd=cwd,
         allow_missing=bool(allow_missing_scopes),
     )
@@

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/agent_loom/memory/README.md
@@
 Common flags:
 
 - `--title`
 - `--id` (advanced; must be link-safe)
 - `--tag` (repeatable; comma-separated ok)
 - `--alias` (repeatable)
 - `--scope` (repeatable)
+- `--command` (shorthand for `--scope command:...`)
 - `--visibility` shared|personal|ephemeral
 - `--status` active|deprecated
 - `--folder` subfolder under visibility root
 - `--body` (string; use `--body -` to read stdin)
 - `--interactive` open editor for body
@@
 Metadata flags:
 
 - `--title`
 - `--tag` / `--remove-tag` / `--clear-tags`
 - `--alias` / `--remove-alias` / `--clear-aliases`
 - `--scope` / `--remove-scope` / `--clear-scopes`
+- `--command` (shorthand for adding `--scope command:...`)
 - `--visibility` shared|personal|ephemeral (moves file)
 - `--status` active|deprecated
 - `--allow-missing-scopes`
@@
 Notes:
 
 - `file` and `folder` are repo-relative when in a git repo.
 - `--allow-missing-scopes` bypasses file existence checks.
-- `--command` is shorthand for `--scope command:...`.
+- `--command` is shorthand for `--scope command:...` (supported by `add`, `edit`, and `recall`).
@@
 ### Capture command output
 
 ```
-pytest -q 2>&1 | loom memory add --title "pytest failure" --tag test --scope command:pytest
-git status -sb | loom memory add --title "status snapshot" --tag git --scope command:"git status"
+pytest -q 2>&1 | loom memory add --title "pytest failure" --tag test --command "pytest -q"
+git status -sb | loom memory add --title "status snapshot" --tag git --command "git status"
 ```
*** End Patch

 class TestMemoryCliUx(unittest.TestCase):
@@
     def test_add_positional_title_rewrites(self) -> None:
         norm = memory_cli_mod._normalize_argv(["add", "Hello"])
         args = memory_cli_mod.build_parser().parse_args(norm)
         self.assertEqual(str(getattr(args, "title", "") or ""), "Hello")
+
+    def test_add_command_flag_parses(self) -> None:
+        norm = memory_cli_mod._normalize_argv(
+            ["add", "Hello", "--command", "uv run pytest"]
+        )
+        args = memory_cli_mod.build_parser().parse_args(norm)
+        self.assertEqual(str(getattr(args, "command", "") or ""), "uv run pytest")
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/agent_loom/compound/docs.py
@@
 **Non-negotiables:**
 - Keep skills small, specific, and triggerable from the `description`. Use "when [condition], then [action]" format when possible.
 - Prefer updating an existing skill over creating a near-duplicate.
 - Never put secrets into skills, memos, or observations.
+- Non-trivial work is ticketed: create/update a Loom ticket before implementing, and keep it updated as you work.
+- Use Loom memory intentionally:
+  - At the start of a task, recall relevant context (prefer `loom memory recall ... --context --format prompt`).
+  - When you learn something reusable, persist a scoped note (prefer `command:` / `file:` scopes).
 
 **Where things live:**
 - Boundary: `.loom/**` is Loom-owned durable evidence + compiled state; `.opencode/**` is OpenCode integration + runtime artifacts (including skills).
 - Skills: `.opencode/skills/<name>/SKILL.md`
 - Instincts: `.loom/compound/instincts.json` (index at `.loom/compound/INSTINCTS.md`)
*** End Patch

*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts
@@
 const PRIME_ON_START = (process.env.COMPOUND_PRIME_ON_START ?? "0") !== "0";
 const REFRESH_ON_START = (process.env.COMPOUND_REFRESH_ON_START ?? "0") !== "0";
+
+const NUDGE_INJECT_ENABLED = (process.env.COMPOUND_NUDGE_INJECT ?? "1") !== "0";
+const NUDGE_TOOL_ENABLED = (process.env.COMPOUND_NUDGE_TOOL ?? "1") !== "0";
+const NUDGE_TOOL_COOLDOWN_SECONDS = intEnv(
+  "COMPOUND_NUDGE_TOOL_COOLDOWN_SECONDS",
+  45
+);
+
+const TURN_REMINDER = [
+  "<reminder>",
+  "Loom protocol:",
+  "- Non-trivial work: create/update a Loom ticket before implementing.",
+  "- Recall memory when relevant: loom memory recall \"<query>\" --context --format prompt.",
+  "- When you learn something reusable, save a scoped note (prefer --command or file: scopes).",
+  "</reminder>",
+].join("\n");
*** End Patch

instinct_candidates.action: Treat the plugin as having a source-of-truth and a distributed copy: update both copies in the same change, or consolidate to a single source and add a deterministic sync step + a check (e.g., CI/test that asserts the two files are identical) so drift can’t happen silently.
instinct_candidates.title: Keep distributed opencode plugin in sync
instinct_candidates.trigger: When editing `.opencode/plugins/compound_engineering.ts` or any opencode plugin that is also shipped under `src/agent_loom/**/opencode/plugins/`

instinct_candidates.action: Add or update a focused CLI UX test that asserts exit code and a small set of stable substrings/lines (not full output), covering both success and failure cases; avoid brittle assertions on whitespace, ordering, or environment-specific paths unless required.
instinct_candidates.title: Add CLI UX regression tests with output changes
instinct_candidates.trigger: When changing any CLI command behavior (stdout/stderr text, exit codes, help output, error messages)

instinct_candidates.action: Update subsystem README/help docs alongside the CLI wiring, and factor logic into a testable core module so UX tests can stay thin; ensure docs mention common workflows and error modes, and add at least one UX test covering the new path.
instinct_candidates.title: Keep docs + core aligned with new subcommands
instinct_candidates.trigger: When introducing a new subcommand or expanding a subsystem CLI (e.g., `loom memory ...`)

skill_candidates.body:
# Sync Distributed Opencode Plugin

Use this when a plugin exists in two places:
- `.opencode/plugins/<name>.ts` (dev copy)
- `src/agent_loom/**/opencode/plugins/<name>.ts` (shipped/distributed copy)

## Steps

1. Identify the two plugin paths that must match.
2. Make the change once, then immediately apply the exact same change to the other copy.
3. Verify they are identical (byte-for-byte) before moving on.
4. Add/keep a regression check that fails if the two files diverge.
5. Run repo gates: `uv run ruff check .` and `uv run basedpyright`, then the relevant tests.

## Guardrails

- Do not rely on memory that "it was copied earlier"; always diff the two files.
- Prefer a deterministic sync/check over human process when possible.
skill_candidates.description: Procedure to prevent drift between `.opencode/plugins/*` and the shipped copy under `src/agent_loom/**/opencode/plugins/*`.
skill_candidates.name: sync-distributed-opencode-plugin
